items.mistake: Differentiating the outer function at x instead of at g(x)
items.fix: Evaluate f′ at the inner output: use f′(g(x)), not f′(x).
items.why: The outer rate of change depends on the intermediate value u = g(x).
items.example: For sin(x²), use cos(x²)·2x, not cos(x)·2x.

items.mistake: Forgetting to multiply by the inner derivative
items.fix: Always include g′(x) as the second factor: (f∘g)′ = f′(g(x))·g′(x).
items.why: The inner step scales how much x affects u; omitting g′(x) gives wrong backprop through early layers.
items.example: d/dx e^{2x} = e^{2x}·2, not e^{2x} alone.

items.mistake: Treating backprop as unrelated to the chain rule
items.fix: Map each backward step to (upstream gradient) × (local Jacobian or derivative).
items.why: Conceptual clarity helps debug custom layers and shape mismatches in gradients.
items.example: If ∂L/∂z is wrong by a factor of x, check whether the chain through z = wx omitted the ∂z/∂w = x term.
